prompt: I have an HTTPS connection called my-api pointing to a REST endpoint. I want to create a processor that uses my-api as the source to read data from the API and write it to my-atlas cluster.

assertions.name: explains_https_not_source
assertions.description: Explains that HTTPS connections CANNOT be used as $source
assertions.reason: HTTPS is not a streaming source — it is a request/response protocol and cannot be polled as a $source

assertions.name: no_https_in_source
assertions.description: Does NOT provide a pipeline with an HTTPS connection in $source

assertions.name: suggests_alternative_source
assertions.description: Suggests a valid source type: Kafka topic or Atlas Cluster change stream

assertions.name: explains_https_capabilities
assertions.description: Explains what HTTPS connections CAN do: mid-pipeline $https enrichment or as a sink

common_mistakes.error: Processor fails immediately — HTTPS is not a valid $source type
common_mistakes.fix: Use Kafka or Atlas Cluster as $source, then use $https mid-pipeline to enrich with the API

correct_pattern.description: If you want to enrich with an HTTP API, use it mid-pipeline

skill_reference: SKILL.md: 'HTTPS connections as $source — HTTPS is for $https enrichment only, not streaming ingestion'
